$comment: Embedded Flink SQL highlighting — Layer 1 (TextMate injection) of the embedded-sql-highlighting capability (vscode-tier-2-feature-9). Heuristic, offline, server-independent: re-scopes the interiors of recognized SQL-context props/components into SQL scopes. Anchored to the SQL-context shapes the shared registry (packages/language-server/src/sql/context-registry.ts) defines; the keyword/function/type lists below mirror packages/language-server/src/sql/vocabulary.ts by hand (a static grammar cannot import TS). Unrecognized fragments (computed/interpolated values) keep ordinary string scoping. The LSP semantic-tokens layer overrides this within its reported ranges.

scalar-attribute.$comment: Filter/Query.Where/Query.Having/Qualify `condition`, Join* `on`, RawSQL `sql`: a scalar SQL string attribute. The injection takes over from the prop name through the closing quote and re-scopes the interior.

select-object.$comment: Map/Aggregate `select={{ col: "<sql>" }}` and Query.Select `columns={{ ... }}`: object whose values are SQL projections. Re-scope each double-quoted value's interior.

validate-expression-object.$comment: Validate `rules={{ expression: { rule: "<sql>" } }}`: only the `expression` sub-object's values are SQL.

watermark-expression.$comment: Schema watermark `{ watermark: { expression: "<sql>" } }`: the scalar `expression` value is SQL. `column` (a bare name) is intentionally not matched.

sql-string-value.$comment: A double-quoted object value whose interior is SQL (used inside select/validate object blocks). A non-string (computed) value is left untouched.

sql-body.$comment: The SQL token vocabulary, applied inside a recognized SQL fragment. Each token carries its standard SQL scope (so stock/custom themes color it via the `keyword`/`support.function`/`storage.type`/… prefix, zero config) plus a trailing `meta.embedded.flinkreactor-sql` marker — the rightmost/most-specific scope — that lets the extension neutralize ONLY FlinkReactor's grammar coloring when `flinkReactor.sql.highlighting` excludes TextMate, without affecting real .sql files. Ordering: comments and SQL string literals first (so their contents are not mis-tokenized), then type/function/keyword (disjoint, vocabulary-driven so DECIMAL(…) stays a type), then operators and numbers. Everything unmatched keeps the enclosing string scope — the graceful plain-string fallback.
